item.title: 1896 – Denver Directory – Entry For Philip Dauth
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Dauth
item.description: The post 1896 – Denver Directory – Entry For Philip Dauth appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.
item.content:encoded:
1896 – Denver Directory – Entry For Philip Dauth

Transcription
"Dauth Philip, wirewkr, 811 7th av W."

People
Philip Dauth
Louis Dauth

Details
Date: 1896
Location: USA, Colorado, Denver
Source: Denver Directory [USA, Colorado, Denver], 1896, p. 40 [Section]

item.title: 1892-08-09 – The Herald Democrat – Louis and Philip Dauth Ride From Glenwood Springs
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Dauth
item.description: The post 1892-08-09 – The Herald Democrat – Louis and Philip Dauth Ride From Glenwood Springs appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.
item.content:encoded:
1892-08-09 – The Herald Democrat – Louis and Philip Dauth Ride From Glenwood Springs

Transcription
The Reading Commandery
"...The pilgrims and the names of the cars they occupy are as follows...Car "England"...Lewis Dauth...J.P. Dauth"

People
Philip Dauth
Louis Dauth

Details
Date: 1892-08-09
Location: USA, Colorado, Leadville
Comments: This is the only article that refers to Philip Dauth as J.P Dauth
Source: "The Reading Commandery." The Herald Democrat [USA, Colorado, Leadville], 1892-08-09, p. 8
Permalink: https://dauthfamily.github.io/article/1892-08-09-the-herald-democrat-louis-and-philip-dauth-ride-from-glenwood-springs/

The post 1892-08-09 – The Herald Democrat – Louis and Philip Dauth Ride From Glenwood Springs appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.

item.title: 1882-09-30 – Fort Collins Courier – Philip Dauth Arrives In Colorado
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Dauth
item.description: The post 1882-09-30 – Fort Collins Courier – Philip Dauth Arrives In Colorado appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.
item.content:encoded:
1882-09-30 – Fort Collins Courier – Philip Dauth Arrives In Colorado

Transcription
"Louis Dauth left for Denver this evening to meet his brother, who has just arrived from Germany. He will make his brother quite a visit in this city."

People
Philip Dauth
Louis Dauth

Details
Date: 1882-09-30
Location: USA, Colorado, Fort Collins
Source: Fort Collins Courier [USA, Colorado, Fort Collins], 1882-09-30, p. 3
Permalink: https://dauthfamily.github.io/article/1882-09-30-fort-collins-courier-philip-dauth-arrives-in-colorado/

The post 1882-09-30 – Fort Collins Courier – Philip Dauth Arrives In Colorado appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.

item.title: 1882-08-28 – Daily Evening Courier – Phillip Dauth Traveling to Fort Collins
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Dauth
item.description: The post 1882-08-28 – Daily Evening Courier – Phillip Dauth Traveling to Fort Collins appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.
item.content:encoded:
1882-08-28 – Daily Evening Courier – Phillip Dauth Traveling to Fort Collins

Transcription
"Mr. Louis Dauth is expecting a younger brother of his to arrive in Fort Collins at any day. The young man left Hesse-Darmstadt about three months since, and has been in New Jersey since his arrival on the shores of America."

People
Philip Dauth
Louis Dauth

Details
Date: 1882-08-28
Location: USA, Colorado, Fort Collins
Source: Daily Evening Courier [USA, Colorado, Fort Collins], 1882-08-28
Permalink: https://dauthfamily.github.io/article/1882-08-28-daily-evening-courier-phillip-dauth-traveling-to-fort-collins/

The post 1882-08-28 – Daily Evening Courier – Phillip Dauth Traveling to Fort Collins appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.

item.title: 1884-09-25 – Fort Collins Courier – Louis Dauth Delegate To Democratic Convention
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Dauth
item.description: The post 1884-09-25 – Fort Collins Courier – Louis Dauth Delegate To Democratic Convention appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.
item.content:encoded:
1884-09-25 – Fort Collins Courier – Louis Dauth Delegate To Democratic Convention

Transcription
"Mr. Louis Dauth, a delegate to the state Democratic convention which convenes on Wednesday, arrived in Denver yesterday from Fort Collins. Mr. Dauth has a wide acquaintance among the Germans of Denver and the state, and states that they are solid for the Democratic ticket. They are heartily wearied of the corruption and the political abuses now in vogue in the Republican party. The delegation is solid for the renomination of Governor Grant. - Denver News "

People
Louis Dauth

Details
Date: 1884-09-25
Location: USA, Colorado, Fort Collins
Source: Fort Collins Courier [USA, Colorado, Fort Collins], 1884-09-25, p. 5
Permalink: https://dauthfamily.github.io/article/1884-09-25-fort-collins-courier-louis-dauth-delegate-to-democratic-convention/

The post 1884-09-25 – Fort Collins Courier – Louis Dauth Delegate To Democratic Convention appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.

item.title: 1886-02-18 – Fort Collins Courier – Louis Dauth Returns From Germany
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Dauth
item.description: The post 1886-02-18 – Fort Collins Courier – Louis Dauth Returns From Germany appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.
item.content:encoded:
1886-02-18 – Fort Collins Courier – Louis Dauth Returns From Germany

Transcription
"Louis Dauth returned, to-day, from his visit to Westhofen, Germany. The faderland evidently agrees with Mr. Dauth's health, for he is looking well after his long absence."

People
Louis Dauth

Details
Date: 1886-02-18
Location: USA, Colorado, Fort Collins
Source: Fort Collins Courier [USA, Colorado, Fort Collins], 1886-02-18, p. 8
Permalink: https://dauthfamily.github.io/article/1886-02-18-fort-collins-courier-louis-dauth-returns-from-germany/

The post 1886-02-18 – Fort Collins Courier – Louis Dauth Returns From Germany appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.

item.title: 1885-11-26 – Fort Collins Courier – Louis Dauth Travels To Germany
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Dauth
item.description: The post 1885-11-26 – Fort Collins Courier – Louis Dauth Travels To Germany appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.
item.content:encoded:
1885-11-26 – Fort Collins Courier – Louis Dauth Travels To Germany
An article describing Louis Dauth's experience in the Franco-Prussian War and his business in Colorado.

Transcription
Bon Voyage
"Ex-Alderman Louis Dauth left Tuesday morning on a three months' excursion and visit to his old home in the Fatherland, which he left thirteen years ago, just after the close of the Franco-Prussian war. Mr. Dauth was one of Emperor William’s gallant soldiers, serving with distinction and honor under the Crown prince, and participating in nearly every one of the hard fought battles of that short but blood war. He came to America in 1872 and after spending a few months on the seaboard started for Colorado, where he has been ever since, with exception of about six months spent in Wyoming. He came to Fort Collins in October, 1877 and with a capital of less than $300 engaged in the bakery and grocery business in the little building now occupied by McGowan & Fuller. He has been very prosperous in all his undertakings and is now a leading and one of our most substantial and highly respected business men and citizens, admired by all for his sterling honesty and his excellent social and business qualifications. Mr. Dauth has well earned a play spell and it is the wish of all who know him that he may experience an enjoyable season. His family will remain with relatives in Denver during his absence. Mr. Adolph Donath, of the Big Thompson valley, another one of Larimer county’s popular adopted citizens, will accompany Alderman Dauth on a similar errand."

People
Louis Dauth

Details
Date: 1885-11-26
Location: USA, Colorado, Fort Collins
Source: "Bon Voyage." Fort Collins Courier [USA, Colorado, Fort Collins], 1885-11-26, p. 1
Permalink: https://dauthfamily.github.io/article/1885-11-26-fort-collins-courier-louis-dauth-travels-to-germany/

The post 1885-11-26 – Fort Collins Courier – Louis Dauth Travels To Germany appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.

item.title: 1883-04-16 – The Daily Express – Louis Dauth Alderman of Third Ward
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Dauth
item.description: The post 1883-04-16 – The Daily Express – Louis Dauth Alderman of Third Ward appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.
item.content:encoded:
1883-04-16 – The Daily Express – Louis Dauth Alderman of Third Ward

Transcription
OLD AND NEW
"The persons taking this oath were Mayer C. Stover and William R. Akers, of the First ward; Edgar R Barkley and Louis W. Welch, Second ward; Joseph R. Wills and Louis Dauth, Third ward;"

People
Louis Dauth

Details
Date: 1883-04-16
Location: USA, Colorado, Fort Collins
Source: "OLD AND NEW." The Daily Express [USA, Colorado, Fort Collins], 1883-04-16
Permalink: https://dauthfamily.github.io/article/1883-04-16-the-daily-express-louis-dauth-alderman-of-third-ward/

item.title: 1883-03-15 – Fort Collins Courier – Louis Dauth Wearing War Medals
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Dauth
item.description: The post 1883-03-15 – Fort Collins Courier – Louis Dauth Wearing War Medals appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.
item.content:encoded:
1883-03-15 – Fort Collins Courier – Louis Dauth Wearing War Medals

Transcription
"Louis Dauth, wears two medals-one of steel, the other of brass, -conferred upon him in the "faderland" in 1871 for services in the Franco-Prussian war."

People
Louis Dauth

Details
Date: 1883-03-15
Location: USA, Colorado, Fort Collins
Source: Fort Collins Courier [USA, Colorado, Fort Collins], 1883-03-15, p. 4
Permalink: https://dauthfamily.github.io/article/1883-03-15-fort-collins-courier-louis-dauth-wearing-war-medals/

The post 1883-03-15 – Fort Collins Courier – Louis Dauth Wearing War Medals appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.
